skill: Data Representation — reading values and trends from a graph or table
source: Derived from error patterns across the official ACT practice test (data/raw/act/)

mistakeCategories.name: Read the value off the wrong axis, row, or trial
mistakeCategories.description: Student reads the correct figure but from an adjacent row/column or the wrong variable.
mistakeCategories.studentExample: Asked for Trial 2 at 30°C, reads the Trial 2 value at 40°C.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Give the value from an adjacent row/column or a neighboring condition in the same figure.

mistakeCategories.name: Reversed the direction of the trend
mistakeCategories.description: Student identifies that a quantity changes but states increasing for decreasing (or vice-versa).
mistakeCategories.studentExample: Values fall as temperature rises; student says 'increases'.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: State the opposite trend direction (increasing vs decreasing) from what the data shows.

mistakeCategories.name: Quoted a max/min instead of the asked value
mistakeCategories.description: Student reports the largest or smallest value in the dataset rather than the specific one requested.
mistakeCategories.studentExample: Asked for the value at a midpoint, gives the peak value.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Give the maximum or minimum value in the figure instead of the specific requested point.

mistakeCategories.name: Correct number, wrong unit or order of magnitude
mistakeCategories.description: Student reads the value correctly but mis-states the unit or scale.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Quote the correct digits but with the wrong unit or order of magnitude from the axis label.
